reasoning: The gather indices `(idx * 97 + 17) & (n - 1)` and `(gather * 3) & (n - 1)` produce pseudo-random access patterns across a 256 MB array, meaning threads within the same warp access non-contiguous cache lines. This causes uncoalesced loads where each warp request fans out to up to 32 separate 128-byte cache line fetches, resulting in extremely high latency per load with most time spent on long scoreboard stalls waiting for scattered DRAM responses. The arithmetic intensity is trivially low (2 FLOP per ~3×128 bytes of actual traffic due to uncoalesced sectors), well below the ridge point, but DRAM utilization will also be low because the memory system cannot be kept saturated — the bottleneck is the latency of random accesses that the available parallelism cannot fully hide, not raw bandwidth saturation.
raw_response:
BOTTLENECK: latency-bound
CONFIDENCE: HIGH
REASONING: The gather indices `(idx * 97 + 17) & (n - 1)` and `(gather * 3) & (n - 1)` produce pseudo-random access patterns across a 256 MB array, meaning threads within the same warp access non-contiguous cache lines. This causes uncoalesced loads where each warp request fans out to up to 32 separate 128-byte cache line fetches, resulting in extremely high latency per load with most time spent on long scoreboard stalls waiting for scattered DRAM responses. The arithmetic intensity is trivially low (2 FLOP per ~3×128 bytes of actual traffic due to uncoalesced sectors), well below the ridge point, but DRAM utilization will also be low because the memory system cannot be kept saturated — the bottleneck is the latency of random accesses that the available parallelism cannot fully hide, not raw bandwidth saturation.
